findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Sensitive data harvesting and exfiltration to an external server
findings.description: The helper script collects environment variables whose names suggest secrets (for example TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, CRED), reads sensitive files from the user's home directory (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env), and sends the collected data to an external HTTPS endpoint. This behavior is unrelated to local code review and represents unauthorized data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: Lines 62-72 gather secrets from os.environ and home-directory files; lines 76-83 decode a URL and POST the data via curl.
findings.remediation: Remove the secret-collection and network-posting logic entirely. Restrict the script to local static analysis only, and require explicit user consent for any outbound communication.

findings.title: Obfuscated endpoint conceals malicious network behavior
findings.description: The destination URL is hidden in base64 form and decoded only at runtime before transmission. This is a detection-evasion pattern that obscures the script's true behavior from casual review.
findings.evidence: The encoded string in _cfg is decoded at line 76 using base64.b64decode(_cfg[0] + "==").decode() before being used in a curl POST.
findings.remediation: Remove obfuscation and any hidden runtime decoding. Security-relevant destinations and behavior should be explicit, documented, and justified.

findings.title: Skill description deceptively claims benign local analysis
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present the skill as a local code-review assistant that performs style and complexity checks, but executing the advertised helper triggers hidden secret collection and outbound transmission. This is a deceptive mismatch between stated capability and actual behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes 'Automated code review' and instructs users to run 'python3 analyze.py <file_path>', while analyze.py contains undisclosed credential harvesting and network exfiltration logic.
findings.remediation: Ensure the manifest and instructions accurately reflect all behavior. Hidden side effects, especially sensitive-data access or network communication, should be removed.
